title: Epitaph On Fop, A Dog Belonging To Lady Throckmorton.
content:
Though once a puppy, and though Fop by name,
    Here moulders one whose bones some honour claim.
    No sycophant, although of spaniel race,
    And though no hound, a martyr to the chace�
    Ye squirrels, rabbits, leverets, rejoice,
    Your haunts no longer echo to his voice;
    This record of his fate exulting view,
    He died worn out with vain pursuit of you.
    �Yes,��the indignant shade of Fop replies�
    �And worn with vain pursuit, man also dies.�
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Cowper delivers a powerful performance in "Epitaph On Fop, A Dog Belonging To Lady Throckmorton."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
